steps.title: Factor out the common term
steps.content:
Each term has a common factor of $2x$:
$$
2x^3+46x^2+240x=2x(x^2+23x+120)
$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Find two numbers that multiply to $120$ and add to $23$. Those numbers are $8$ and $15$, so
$$
x^2+23x+120=(x+8)(x+15)
$$

steps.title: Choose the smallest factor value
steps.content: The factors of the form $x+b$ are $x+8$ and $x+15$. The smallest possible positive value of $b$ is $8$. The answer is 8.
